stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 64.2 % (within-trial site SD = 3.2). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 62.6 % (within-trial site SD = 6.3). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 62.5 % (within-trial site SD = 4.0). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 60.9 % (within-trial site SD = 5.3). trial site E has 5 observations with mean 73.2 % (within-trial site SD = 7.2). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.5 and 8.7 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 62.6 % (shrunk from raw 62.5). Range [62.5, 62.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 3.5 to 8.7. Pooled mean: 64.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 64.3 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=5.8: B = 0.955.
Interval [62.5, 62.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (62.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (64.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
